events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess compliance for flashing & weeps in a commercial project. The observed condition: weep holes missing/blocked at bottom course. Work stage: rough-complete. Reference: TMS 402, BIA tech notes. We need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, etc.

Weep holes are required in masonry walls to allow moisture to drain. TMS 402 (Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures) and BIA tech notes (Brick Industry Association) specify that weep holes must be provided at the base of walls, above flashing, to drain water. Missing or blocked weep holes would be non-compliant. This is a defect that could lead to moisture damage, efflorescence, freeze-thaw damage, etc. So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Missing weeps can cause water accumulation, leading to structural issues over time, so I'd say high. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective (weeps missing/blocked) and non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 90%? The wall is roughed in but missing weeps, so not fully complete. I'll estimate 90. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Weep holes missing or blocked at bottom course, preventing drainage." Actions: "Install or clear weep holes per TMS 402 and BIA tech notes." Rationale: "Weep holes are required by code to drain moisture from masonry walls; missing/blocked weeps can cause water damage and structural issues." References: "TMS 402, BIA Tech Notes 7, 21".

I'll output JSON accordingly.

  "references": ["TMS 402", "BIA Tech Notes 7, 21"]
}
